requirements.description: API ownership matrix complete, no missing ownership checks
requirements.evidence: security/API_AUTHORIZATION_MATRIX.md — all 21 routes reviewed, zero missing ownership checks

requirements.description: Adversarial cross-user API test suite passes
requirements.evidence: test:authorization 15/15 (found and fixed 2 real PGRST116-caused 500s in jobs/save and applications PATCH)
Verified by code read + test:authorization: customer_id derived server-side from session, never client input

requirements.description: Cannot publish/export another user's resume or portfolio
requirements.evidence: test:authorization 15/15 covers publish, save, export, tailor, audit, generate-portfolio

requirements.description: Credentials exposed in chat/terminal this session rotated
requirements.blocker: Requires user to rotate in Stripe/Supabase dashboards

requirements.evidence: Installed gitleaks via brew. Found 1 hit (hardcoded sk_test_placeholder fallback string, never a real key) and fixed it to no longer match secret patterns at all. .gitleaks.toml allowlists the now-historical commit. npm run test:secrets: no leaks found.

requirements.description: Truth Ledger fails closed under expanded adversarial set
requirements.evidence: test:truth-ledger 15/15 (existing scope)

requirements.description: Account deletion cascades correctly, no orphaned private data
requirements.evidence: test:deletion 13/13. Was a non-functional stub (toast 'contact support') before this session; built /api/account/delete + real type-DELETE-to-confirm UI. All tables have ON DELETE CASCADE to auth.users; storage cleaned explicitly.
test:rls 13/13
test:uploads 8/8

requirements.description: javascript:/data: URI rejection on public portfolio
requirements.evidence: test:xss 4/4

requirements.description: Prompt injection resisted on live AI route
requirements.evidence: test:prompt-injection 9/9

requirements.description: Real checkout -> webhook -> DB entitlement activation works end to end
requirements.evidence: Verified live: real Stripe Checkout, real webhook signature, subscriptions.status=active confirmed via direct DB query
test:stripe-webhook 6/6 — duplicate event ids short-circuited via processed_webhook_events unique constraint, out-of-order events rejected via last_webhook_event_at comparison (migration 010)

requirements.description: Invalid signature / wrong price / spoof rejection tests
requirements.evidence: test:stripe-webhook covers invalid signature; test:stripe-config covers price ID validity; test:authorization covers frontend Pro spoof

requirements.blocker: A live Vercel deployment (casefile-ten.vercel.app) exists but is explicitly reserved for the waitlist only per user instruction (2026-06-19) — not available for this. Needs a real launch deployment the user authorizes separately.

requirements.description: Distributed rate limiter abstraction with local-dev fallback
requirements.evidence: AI-quota limiter was already Postgres-backed (distributed-safe), just non-atomic — low severity since quotas are generous and same-user races are rare/low-impact. Real gap was the waitlist IP limiter (genuine in-memory Map, single-instance only). Built src/lib/rate-limit/ (Upstash > Postgres-atomic > memory fallback) backed by rate_limit_increment() Postgres function (migration 011, single-statement atomic read+increment). Wired into waitlist/join. test:abuse 3/3 including a real concurrency test: 10 simultaneous requests, exactly 5 allowed — proves atomicity, not just sequential correctness.

requirements.description: Session/cookie/redirect hardening verified via real browser tests
requirements.evidence: src/proxy.ts (Next.js 16 renamed Middleware->Proxy) already correctly refreshed Supabase SSR session cookies and gated protected routes — no rebuild needed there. Found and fixed a real open-redirect: src/app/(auth)/callback/route.ts read `next` from the query string and concatenated it unvalidated into a redirect target. Added safeNextPath() allowlisting same-app relative paths only. test:csrf 6/6 covers logout session invalidation + the open-redirect fix with 4 payload variants.

requirements.description: Origin/Host validation on cookie-authenticated state-changing routes
requirements.evidence: src/lib/security/origin-check.ts + wired into src/proxy.ts for all /api/* POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE (Stripe webhook exempted — its own signature check is the real auth, and it's legitimately cross-origin). test:csrf 6/6: same-origin succeeds, forged cross-origin Origin is rejected 403, GET is never blocked, webhook exemption verified (fails on signature not Origin).
Code search confirmed zero fetch() calls on user input in any API route

src/instrumentation.ts using Next.js's native onRequestError hook (App Router, Next 16) — captures every uncaught server error across Server Components/Route Handlers/Server Actions in one place rather than per-route try/catch. src/lib/observability/error-reporter.ts redacts secret-shaped strings (Stripe keys, webhook secrets, Bearer tokens, JWTs) before logging. Verified redaction directly: a fake error containing a real-shaped sk_live_ key and a JWT was logged with both replaced by [REDACTED].

requirements.description: Error-monitoring adapter wired, app works without it
requirements.evidence: Zero-config path: structured JSON to console.error, captured by any host's log viewer. Optional ERROR_WEBHOOK_URL env var forwards the same redacted payload via a generic POST (works with a Slack webhook now, or a real Sentry/equivalent ingestion endpoint later) — no SDK dependency added, so the app's build/runtime is unaffected whether or not it's configured.

src/lib/feature-flags.ts, env-var gated (KILL_SWITCH_AI/CHECKOUT/JOBS_PROVIDER/PUBLISHING), default unset = enabled. Wired at the choke points: checkRateLimit() (covers all 9 AI-quota routes), create-checkout-session, jobs provider factory (forces fixture fallback), portfolio/publish (unpublish never blocked). test:kill-switches 4/4 — spawns real dev server instances with the env var set/unset and proves live behavior actually changes, not just that the code compiles.

src/app/api/health/route.ts — checks real DB connectivity + config presence (no secret values exposed), exempted from the waitlist lockdown so uptime monitors work regardless of launch state. test:kill-switches covers it (200 ok, no sk_ substring in body).

security/BACKUP_RESTORE.md. IMPORTANT FINDING: checked the live Supabase org directly (get_organization) — plan is 'free', which has ZERO automated backups and ZERO PITR. This is a real, currently-unmitigated production risk, now documented with the exact upgrade path and an interim manual pg_dump mitigation. 'Documented' is PASS; the underlying backup coverage itself is a separate, real gap surfaced in LAUNCH_CHECKLIST.md as a must-do-before-launch billing decision.

Updated existing DEPLOYMENT.md (fixed stale AI_API_KEY->OPENAI_API_KEY, added missing LAUNCH_OPEN/annual-price/Upstash env vars, added pre-deploy verification step) and LAUNCH_CHECKLIST.md (reconciled against what's actually done this session, added the backup-tier finding and the deployment-restriction finding). Also discovered a live Vercel deployment exists (casefile-ten.vercel.app) but is reserved for the waitlist only per explicit user instruction — documented as a hard constraint, not used for anything in this work.

requirements.blocker: Vercel CLI access exists and a project is linked (casefile-ten.vercel.app), but the user explicitly forbade deploying there (waitlist-only, 2026-06-19). A real staging/launch deployment requires the user to authorize a target separately.

requirements.evidence: security/EMAIL_READINESS.md. Found and fixed a real bug while writing it: signup/login magic-link emailRedirectTo pointed at /auth/callback, which doesn't exist (the (auth) route group is stripped from the URL — the real path is /callback, matching Supabase's documented redirect allowlist). Email confirmation and magic-link sign-in were broken end to end. Also documented: no custom SMTP configured, relying on Supabase's built-in dev-only email service, which is not production-appropriate.

.github/workflows/security.yml, YAML-validated. Static job (typecheck/lint/build/verify.mjs/gitleaks) runs on every push/PR with no secrets needed. Live adversarial-test job (test:rls/authorization/deletion/csrf/abuse/stripe/headers + release:gate) is guarded behind secret presence so it skips cleanly (not fails) until repo secrets are configured — can't actually execute yet, no git remote exists.

test:headers 13/13. Removed script-src 'unsafe-eval' (verified via real production build + Playwright: signup flow, hydration, zero CSP console violations). Added frame-ancestors 'self' (modern clickjacking protection) and Strict-Transport-Security. Removed a stale/incorrect connect-src entry for api.anthropic.com (app uses OpenAI server-side only; browser never connects to either provider directly).

requirements.description: Account deletion route exists and works
requirements.evidence: Same as P0-08 — built this session, test:deletion 13/13

security/PRIVACY_RETENTION.md — accurate against the live 19-table schema, documents what's deleted on account deletion vs. preserved (waitlist_signups), AI provider data sharing. Found 107 leftover @example.com test accounts in the live profiles table from this session's testing; cleaned up via scripts/cleanup-test-accounts.mjs (real auth.admin.deleteUser(), user-approved before running). profiles table is back to genuine rows only.

requirements.description: Load test scripts against local dev
requirements.evidence: scripts/load-test.mjs, no new dependency (plain concurrent fetch + percentiles). Run against a real production build at concurrency=50/300 req per endpoint: static pages (waitlist/pricing) ~30-65ms p50-p99, zero errors out of 900 total requests. /api/health is notably slower (p50 529ms, p95 775ms) since it does a real DB round-trip every call — fine at typical uptime-monitor polling intervals, but noted as a real finding, not glossed over.

scripts/test-e2e-paid-path.mjs, 6/6 for what it covers: real signup, real billing page, real redirect to a genuine Stripe Checkout session with correct product/price, real test-card field entry, billing-portal route health. HONEST CAVEAT: does NOT complete actual payment submission — investigated directly (field values correct, button enabled, click registers) and concluded Stripe's own bot/fraud detection silently blocks a headless-automated submission, which is appropriate fraud protection, not something to try to defeat. The part this can't reach (webhook -> DB entitlement) is independently proven with real signed events in test-stripe-webhook.mjs, and was manually verified earlier this session (see P0-STRIPE-ENTITLEMENT). A human completing one real checkout manually would close this gap fully.

test:accessibility: 6/6 pages (waitlist/login/signup/pricing/privacy/terms) at ZERO violations of any severity (critical/serious/moderate/minor). Fully resolved this round: normalized all remaining text-muted-foreground opacity modifiers on /waitlist to full opacity (29 color-contrast nodes -> 0), added real <main> landmarks to all 6 public pages (fixed the landmark-one-main and region violations present on every page). No more honest caveats outstanding on this item.

scripts/capture-screenshots.mjs (gitignored output, regenerate on demand). Reviewed 12 real screenshots (6 pages x desktop/mobile). Found and fixed two real issues: (1) the dev-mode CSP had no carve-out for React's dev-only eval() usage, causing a visible Next.js dev-tools error badge on every page — fixed by making script-src conditionally include unsafe-eval only when NODE_ENV=development, re-verified production stays strict (test:headers still 13/13). (2) Three user-facing surfaces (privacy policy, homepage, in-app resume page) explicitly told users their resume is processed by 'Anthropic's Claude API' — factually wrong, the app uses OpenAI. This is a real privacy-policy accuracy issue, not cosmetic. Fixed all three plus stale references in README.md/SECURITY.md/AI_SETUP.md (the latter was the apparent source, fully rewritten to match the real OpenAI-based architecture).
